seniority.level: Staff / Principal IC (very senior individual contributor)
level_signals: 12+ years of related experience (or 10+ with Master's degree)
Operates autonomously, advises senior leadership and executive staff
Advanced professional certifications expected at this level of seniority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Typically 12+ years of related experience (or 10+ years with a Master's degree), demonstrating a track record of solving unique problems with complex scope.
must_haves.qualifier: or 10+ years with a Master's degree

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Accomplished subject matter expert status in Operational Excellence, with a deep understanding of how the specialization fits into and impacts the larger company objectives.
Proven ability to operate autonomously in setting goals, deciding priorities, and running after problems without the need for direct supervision.
Exceptional ability to maneuver through ambiguity and shifting priorities with ease while engaging and educating stakeholders on the strategic "why".
Minimum Bachelor's Degree in Business Management, Technology, or a related field; advanced professional certifications are expected at this level of seniority.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: PMP, CAPM or SCM certification is preferred

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska

scope.surface: Operational Excellence function across all SoFi products
scope.business_model: B2C financial services / national bank, mobile-first
cross_functional_partners: Cross-functional stakeholders across all SoFi products
core_responsibilities: Advise leadership on the vision and strategy for the organization
Deconstruct extremely large, significantly complex efforts into straightforward implementation strategies
Lead the development of experiments and pilots, making it 'safe to fail' as part of the ideation and invention process
Handle critical escalations that require sensitive solutions, such as those impacting brand trust
Develop and leverage strategic partnerships to align highest-level stakeholders
Actively work to upskill the function and help peers understand complex trade-offs
Assume a leadership role in identifying and mitigating risks at the departmental and cross-functional level

green_flags: High-impact, high-visibility individual contributor role advising executive leadership
Clear articulation of responsibilities and scope
Role spans all SoFi products, offering broad organizational exposure
EEO and San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance commitments noted
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed in JD body despite reference to base pay range
Location type is ambiguous — JD mentions Hawaii/Alaska remote exclusion, implying remote is possible, but no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite designation is stated
Advanced professional certifications are 'expected' at this seniority level but only PMP/CAPM/SCM listed as preferred — creates ambiguity about which certs are truly required
High buzzword density may obscure concrete day-to-day expectations
12+ years minimum experience bar is very high and narrowly defined

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (inferred as 'unclear' — JD references Hawaii/Alaska remote exclusion implying some remote eligibility, but no explicit work model stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred true from Hawaii/Alaska exclusion language)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2C financial services / national bank)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
seniority.level (inferred from years requirement, autonomy signals, and advisory role to executives)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from standard language about final offer based on location)
ambiguities: The JD states 'advanced professional certifications are expected at this level of seniority' under must-haves, but the Nice To Have section lists PMP/CAPM/SCM as 'preferred'. This creates a contradiction — certifications may be simultaneously expected (hard) and merely preferred (soft). Treated the general certification expectation as hard and the specific named certs as soft.
Location type is not explicitly stated. The Hawaii/Alaska exclusion implies remote work is possible, but no hybrid or onsite requirement is mentioned. Treated as 'unclear'.
Compensation range is referenced ('The base pay range for this role is listed below') but no actual numbers appear in the JD content provided.
